records.title: PR size and change-risk analysis gate
records.objective: Add a PR/change-set sizing and risk gate so oversized or high-risk changes are flagged before GitHub promotion.
records.scope:
- Count changed files, changed lines, deleted lines, generated files, and protected-path touches.
- Classify risk by size, file type, subsystem, and protected area.
- Warn or fail based on configured thresholds.
- Emit risk summary into closeout and PR handoff artifacts.
records.owner:
Sentinel + Auditor

Close this issue when the bead is marked done.
records.acceptance:
- [ ] Changed-file and line-count metrics are captured.
- [ ] Protected path touches are detected.
- [ ] Configurable thresholds exist for warn/fail behavior.
- [ ] Large PR warning appears before push/PR creation.
- [ ] Risk result is saved to audit output.

records.title: Security, secrets, and dependency scanning gates
records.objective: Prevent vulnerable code, exposed secrets, and unsafe dependencies from reaching GitHub workflows.
records.scope:
- Secret scanning.
- Dependency vulnerability scanning.
- Container image scanning where applicable.
- Risk scoring in audit output.
records.acceptance:

- [ ] High severity findings fail the gate.
- [ ] Scan results stored as artifacts.
- [ ] Security summary included in closeout report.

records.title: Mirror agent queue to GitHub issues and mutations to PRs (Phase 3)
records.objective: Provide full bidirectional visibility between the internal agent handoff queue and GitHub: queue items surface as issues so humans can track them, and mutations (state changes, closes, PR links) are reflected back on GitHub in near-real time.
records.scope:
- Queue-to-issue mirroring (Phase 3a): already shipped in `scripts/sync_queue_to_github.py` and PR #53.
- Mutation-to-PR mirroring (Phase 3b): when a bead transitions (claim, close, fail), open or update a linked GitHub PR or comment.
- Bidirectional close sync (Phase 3c): closing a GH issue marks the bead done; bead marked done closes the GH issue.
- Dedup guard: re-runs must not create duplicate issues or PRs.
- Audit trail: every sync action appended to `07_LOGS_AND_AUDIT/queue_sync/history.jsonl`.
records.acceptance:
- [ ] `python scripts/sync_queue_to_github.py --execute` creates one GH issue per open queue bead and skips existing ones (no duplicates on re-run).
- [ ] When a bead is claimed or closed, a corresponding GitHub PR or issue comment is created/updated with the new state within one sync cycle.
- [ ] Closing a GH issue tagged `agent-queue` triggers bead done marking (or logs a pending mutation for the next harvest cycle).
- [ ] Running `sync_queue_to_github.py --execute --close-done` closes GH issues whose beads are marked done, without touching open beads.
- [ ] Sync history is appended to `07_LOGS_AND_AUDIT/queue_sync/history.jsonl` with timestamp, action, bead_id, and issue number on every --execute run.
- [ ] intake_issues.py accepts issue #51 as valid (Acceptance checks section present) and seed_issues_to_beads.py places it in the Queue Infrastructure epic.
